is_where_most_people_start_fingers_res: is where most people start — fingers rest on a, s, d, f  ·  j, k, l, ;
button_dyslexia_font_audio_cues_high_c: button. Dyslexia font, audio cues, high-contrast, large-key keyboard, and more — they're the product, not a fallback.
no_timers_no_races_no_streak_punishmen: No timers, no races, no streak punishments. Go at your pace. Pause whenever.
drill_ai_writes_practice_text_at_your_: drill — AI writes practice text at your grade level about anything you care about. Turn on
and_it_will_illustrate_what_you_type: and it will illustrate what you type.
can_change_the_row_of_colored_swatches: can change — the row of colored swatches above switches between 5 visual themes. Or press
a_clinician_or_teacher_can_set_an_iep_: A clinician or teacher can set an IEP goal and export progress from the
unique_calendar_days_with_at_least_one: Unique calendar days with at least one session in the last 30 days. No guilt for days off.
type_or_paste_the_text_plain_letters_n: Type or paste the text. Plain letters, numbers, and common punctuation work best.
this_session_won_t_be_saved_no_pressur: — this session won't be saved. No pressure, no record.
or_press_space_or_enter_to_begin_press: Or press Space or Enter to begin. Press Esc any time during the drill to exit.
non_english_passages_use_ascii_only_no: Non-English passages use ASCII only (no accents / tildes) so standard keyboard keys remain the practice target.
e_g_space_dogs_minecraft_the_ocean_mai: e.g., space, dogs, Minecraft, the ocean, Maine winters
hand_written_passage_sets_that_work_wi: Hand-written passage sets that work without AI. Imported passages join your saved library; same passage week-after-week is a fairer baseline for IEP trend than re-generated text.
recently_generated_passages_drill_any_: Recently generated passages. Drill any of them to reuse. Oldest drops when you generate a 9th.
no_text_loaded_for_this_drill: No text loaded for this drill.
warmup_mode_session_will_not_be_saved: Warmup mode — session will not be saved
each_bar_chars_typed_in_a_10_second_wi: Each bar = chars typed in a 10-second window. Hover for approx WPM.
your_answer_helps_teachers_see_when_th: Your answer helps teachers see when the numbers don't tell the whole story.
how_did_this_session_go_started_slow_p: How did this session go? "Started slow, persisted, used slow-motion mode..."
note_saved_with_this_session_it_will_a: Note saved with this session. It will appear in the IEP report and CSV export.
refine_the_last_image_using_image_to_i: Refine the last image using image-to-image instead of starting from scratch
download_this_image_as_a_png_file: Download this image as a PNG file
retry_the_same_text_to_beat_your_score: Retry the same text to beat your score
shows_the_full_passage_for_this_many_s: Shows the full passage for this many seconds before typing starts. Helps students who benefit from reading first, then producing. The student can skip the countdown at any time.
after_this_many_minutes_of_active_typi: After this many minutes of active typing (paused time not counted), show a gentle reminder to take a break. Fires once per drill. Off by default.
swap_the_whole_look_of_the_tool_high_c: Swap the whole look of the tool. High-contrast mode overrides themes. Kawaii is a light theme; the others are dark variants.
swap_the_highlight_color_used_for_the_: Swap the highlight color used for the current character, buttons, and milestones. For students who find blue too cold or want warmer tones for focus. High-contrast mode overrides this.
prefer_shorter_or_longer_drill_texts_s: Prefer shorter or longer drill texts. "Short" helps students with attention or fatigue; "Long" is for students building endurance. If a drill has no samples matching the preference, falls back to any.
a_soft_pulsing_dot_on_the_drill_screen: A soft pulsing dot on the drill screen marks a steady rhythm. There is NO penalty for going slower — this is a reference, not a deadline. Helpful for motor-planning. Off by default.
this_is_your_space_why_are_you_practic: This is your space. Why are you practicing? What's your target for today? Separate from the clinician's IEP goal.
e_g_typing_feels_hard_now_but_i_m_goin: e.g., "Typing feels hard now, but I'm going to be able to write my own stories." · "I want to email my grandma without my mom helping." · "Faster typing = less homework time."
today_s_goal_is_yours_not_counted_agai: Today's goal is yours — not counted against you if you miss it. Resets automatically tomorrow.
set_an_iep_style_goal_here_it_shows_up: Set an IEP-style goal here. It shows up on the drill screen and pre-fills the progress report. Only needed if you're generating formal progress notes.
use_initials_or_a_pseudonym_alloflow_i: Use initials or a pseudonym — AlloFlow is designed to stay FERPA-safe.
e_g_prefers_home_row_with_large_key_ke: e.g., Prefers home-row with large-key keyboard; reduce errors before increasing speed.
save_the_accommodations_iep_goal_audio: Save the accommodations + IEP goal + audio theme to JSON, or load a profile from another device. Does NOT copy session history — that stays per student.
full_backup_includes_sessions_personal: Full backup includes sessions, personal best, mastery, error heatmap, and both libraries — the whole student record. Use this before clearing browser data or switching devices.
your_personal_growth_path_no_peer_comp: Your personal growth path. No peer comparison — this is just you, over time.
affects_trend_report_csv_skill_tree_st: Affects trend, report, CSV. Skill tree stays all-time.
add_or_edit_a_note_saves_on_blur_or_ct: Add or edit a note. Saves on blur or Ctrl+Enter.
green_improvement_red_regression_gray_: Green Δ = improvement · red = regression · gray = no judgment applied (duration, accommodations count).
top_error_keys_by_drill_all_time: Top error keys by drill · all-time
different_drills_surface_different_wea: Different drills surface different weaknesses. Use this to pick drills that target specific problem keys.
these_celebrate_trying_accommodations_: These celebrate trying accommodations — not hiding them. Using what helps is strength, not weakness.
average_wpm_with_each_accommodation_on: Average WPM with each accommodation on vs off. Aggregate across drill types — drill mix can confound, so use the per-drill breakdown in the report below for high-stakes decisions.
tap_the_report_to_select_all_or_use_th: Tap the report to select all, or use the buttons below.
copy_a_2_4_sentence_plain_language_sum: Copy a 2-4 sentence plain-language summary for a parent email
complete_your_first_drill_to_see_your_: Complete your first drill to see your baseline, trend, and IEP report here.
no_sessions_match_the_current_filter: 🔎 No sessions match the current filter.
move_between_drill_cards_and_nav_butto: Move between drill cards and nav buttons
start_today_s_drill_of_the_day: Start today's drill of the day
on_any_screen_with_a_form: On any screen with a form
everything_in_typing_practice_can_be_r: Everything in Typing Practice can be reached with the keyboard — that's by design for screen-reader users and motor-planning accommodations.
accommodation_toggles_on_the_drill_int: Accommodation toggles on the drill-intro screen are fully keyboard-reachable via Tab — no mouse required for any part of this tool.
optional_game_mode_words_rise_from_the: Optional game mode. Words rise from the bottom; type the top word to clear it. Stack hits the ceiling and the match ends. No leaderboard, no streak guilt — just personal best.
each_theme_has_a_mascot_who_shows_up_i: Each theme has a mascot who shows up in the playfield, on the menu, and in the summary screen. They react to what you're doing — combo, danger, attack, sleep, win, loss.
choose_an_attack_word_to_send_to_the_b: Choose an attack word to send to the bot
attack_ready_pick_a_word_to_send: ⚡ ATTACK READY — pick a word to send
tap_a_card_or_press_1_2_3_to_send_pres: Tap a card OR press 1 / 2 / 3 to send. Press Esc to skip and not send. Your stack is frozen while you choose.
paused_both_stacks_frozen_press_resume: ⏸ Paused — both stacks frozen. Press Resume or Esc to continue.
build_foundation_slow_accurate_beats_f: Build foundation. Slow + accurate beats fast + sloppy.
speed_accuracy_in_productive_band_sust: Speed + accuracy in productive band. Sustainable practice.
high_wpm_achieved_diminishing_returns_: High WPM achieved. Diminishing returns from speed.
speed_exceeded_accuracy_budget_backtra: Speed exceeded accuracy budget. Backtrack to slower target.
five_sliders_tune_your_practice_target: Five sliders tune your practice target. Widget classifies your zone into one of 5 discrete states and renders the WPM-accuracy Pareto frontier. No score on your tuning — the inquiry is in finding sustainable practice points.
pareto_curve_best_accuracy_achievable_: Pareto curve = best accuracy achievable at each WPM. Above = unrealistic; below = room to grow.
hypothesis_free_text_what_is_your_sust: Hypothesis (free text): What is your sustainable target? How does accuracy floor change effective WPM?
set_wpm_80_accuracy_98_realistic_compa: Set WPM=80, accuracy=98. Realistic? Compare to your zone marker.
drop_accuracy_floor_to_80_does_effecti: Drop accuracy floor to 80%. Does effective WPM rise?
pros_sustain_95_at_70_wpm_investigate_: Pros sustain 95%+ at 70+ WPM. Investigate what training paths get there.
a_slower_target_with_high_accuracy_may: A slower target with high accuracy may produce more text per session. Test.
i_understand_the_tradeoff_explain_in_o: I understand the tradeoff — explain in own words
explain_in_your_own_words_how_do_wpm_a: Explain in your own words: how do WPM, accuracy, and momentum interact? What does the Pareto frontier mean for typing practice?
design_note_discrete_5_zone_marker_par: Design note: discrete 5-zone marker; Pareto curve shows tradeoff space; no "correct" target — by design.
